name: Udupi
description: Famous for the Krishna Mutt (matha) founded by Madhvacharya in the 13th century, where the deity is worshipped through a unique silver-plated window. The city is synonymous with Udupi cuisine (especially masala dosa). Nearby beaches (Malpe, St. Mary's Island with columnar basalt formations), and the Manipal educational hub add to its appeal.

name: Shravanabelagola
description: One of the most important Jain pilgrimage sites, home to the 17.3-meter tall Gommateshwara (Bahubali) monolithic statue — the world's tallest Jain monolith, carved from a single block of granite in 983 CE. The biennial Mahamastakabhisheka festival draws millions of devotees for the grand anointment ceremony of the ancient statue.

name: Chikmagalur
description: A scenic hill station in the Mullayanagiri range (highest peak in Karnataka at 1,930 m), famous for its sprawling coffee plantations, misty mountains, and scenic trekking trails. The Baba Budangiri Hills (where coffee was first cultivated in India), Hebbe Falls, and the Kalhatti Falls offer spectacular natural beauty amidst the Western Ghats.

name: Srirangapatna
description: The historic island capital of Tipu Sultan, surrounded by the Kaveri River. The Ranganathaswamy Temple (one of the largest in India), Tipu Sultan's Summer Palace (Daria Daulat Bagh), the Gumbaz (Tipu's tomb), and the Sangam (confluence of the Kaveri and its tributaries) with its 12th-century bridges are major historic and architectural treasures.

name: Pattadakal
description: A UNESCO World Heritage site with a group of 8th-century Chalukya temples that blend the northern Nagara and southern Dravidian architectural styles. The Virupaksha Temple, Mallikarjuna Temple, and the Papanatha Temple are masterpieces of Hindu temple architecture, adorned with exquisite sculptures narrating stories from the Ramayana and Mahabharata.

name: Dandeli
description: An eco-tourism destination on the banks of the Kali River, famous for white-water rafting, kayaking, bird watching, and jungle camping in the Dandeli Wildlife Sanctuary. The Syntheri Rocks (massive limestone monolith), Kavala Caves, and trekking through the lush Western Ghats teak forests offer an adventurous escape into nature.

name: Tiruvannamalai
description: Home to the sacred Annamalaiyar Temple at the base of the holy Arunachala Hill, one of the Pancha Bhoota Stalas (representing the fire element). The 2,600-foot Girivalam path around the hill, the ashram of Ramana Maharshi, and the spectacular Karthigai Deepam festival when a massive fire is lit atop the hill attract spiritual seekers from around the world.

name: Palani Murugan Temple
description: One of the six abodes of Lord Murugan (Arupadai Veedu), situated atop a 150-meter granite hill. The temple is famous for the unique idol of Dandayudhapani (Murugan as a renunciate with a staff), and devotees offer panchamritam (a sweet mixture of five fruits). The hill can be climbed on foot, by cable car, or on the scenic Palani hill railway.

name: Chidambaram Nataraja Temple
description: A legendary temple dedicated to Lord Shiva as Nataraja (the Cosmic Dancer), representing one of the Pancha Bhoota Stalas (space element). The temple is famous for its gold-roofed sanctum, the 108 dance poses (karanas) carved on the gopurams, and the sacred Akasa Lingam (a formless representation of Shiva). The annual Natyanjali Dance Festival celebrates classical dance.

name: Kumbakonam
description: A historic temple town on the Kaveri River, known as the 'City of Temples' with over 1,000 ancient temples. The Mahamaham Lake (where a massive festival is held every 12 years), the Airavateswara Temple (UNESCO, at Darasuram), Nageswaran Temple, Kumbeswarar Temple, and the Adi Kumbeswarar Temple are architectural marvels of Chola and Nayak craftsmanship.

name: Valparai
description: A scenic hill station in the Anamalai Hills at 1,200 meters, surrounded by tea and coffee plantations. The drive up with 40 hairpin bends through the Anamalai Tiger Reserve offers spectacular mountain views. The Balaji Temple, Sholayar Dam viewpoints, Monkey Falls, and the misty Nallamudi Viewpoint make it a serene south Indian hill retreat.

name: Hogenakkal Falls
description: Known as the 'Niagara of South India', where the Kaveri River plunges through a steep rocky gorge, creating multiple cascades and a mesmerizing mist. Coracle (round boat) rides through the turbulent waters, the unique hydrotherapy from the natural showers, and the surrounding Melagiri Hills offer an unforgettable natural experience.

name: Kolhapur
description: The cultural capital of Maharashtra, famous for the Mahalakshmi Temple (one of the Shakti Peethas), the magnificent New Palace (housing a museum with Kolhapur's royal artifacts), Rankala Lake, and the historic Panhala Fort. Kolhapuri chappal (handcrafted leather sandals) and the spicy Kolhapuri cuisine (especially misal and tandoori chicken) are world-renowned.

name: Pandharpur
description: The most important pilgrimage site in Maharashtra, home to the Vithoba Temple (a form of Krishna). The annual Pandharpur Wari (Ashadhi Ekadashi) pilgrimage draws over a million devotees who walk hundreds of kilometers carrying the palkhis of saints Dnyaneshwar and Tukaram. The temple's 13th-century architecture and the Chandrabhaga River ghats are spiritually significant.

name: Trimbakeshwar Temple
description: One of the twelve Jyotirlingas, uniquely featuring a three-faced lingam representing Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva. Located at the源头 of the Godavari River (Gautama Godavari) at 750 meters in the Trimbak hills. The surrounding hills (Brahmagiri), the Kushavarta Kund, and the serene temple architecture make it a deeply spiritual pilgrimage destination.

name: Bhimashankar Temple
description: One of the twelve Jyotirlingas, nestled deep in the Sahyadri hills at 950 meters within the Bhimashankar Wildlife Sanctuary. The ancient temple features Nagara-style architecture, while the surrounding dense forests with giant squirrels, leopards, and over 200 bird species make it a unique blend of spirituality and wildlife. The trek through the sanctuary adds to the experience.

name: Ganpatipule
description: A pristine coastal town on the Konkan coast, famous for the 400-year-old Swayambhu (self-originated) Ganpati Temple on the beach. The 5-km long beach with golden sand and calm waters, the Ganapatipule Lighthouse, and the nearby Bandhavgarh Fort and Jaigad Fort offer a perfect combination of beach relaxation, spiritual tranquility, and historic exploration.

name: Murud-Janjira Fort
description: A unique sea fort built on an island off the Konkan coast, considered one of the strongest marine forts in India. The fort with 19 rounded bastions has 19 gates and has never been conquered in its 800-year history — neither by the Portuguese, British, nor Marathas. Accessible by boat from Murud village, the fort offers stunning Arabian Sea views.

name: Tarkarli Beach
description: A pristine white-sand beach at the confluence of the Karli River and the Arabian Sea, known for some of the best snorkeling and scuba diving in Maharashtra. The clear waters, the 17th-century Sindhudurg Fort (built by Shivaji Maharaj, accessible by boat), and the nearby Devbagh island make it a premier water sports and beach destination on the Konkan coast.

name: Palitana Temples
description: The holiest pilgrimage site for Jains, with over 860 marble temples spread across the Shatrunjaya hill, clustered in groups (tonks) and accessed by climbing 3,800 steps. The main temple dedicated to Adinath (first Tirthankara) is an architectural marvel with intricate marble carvings. The sunrise view from the hilltop is considered one of India's most spectacular pilgrim vistas.

name: Champaner-Pavagadh
description: A UNESCO World Heritage site, featuring a unique fusion of Hindu and Islamic architecture from the 15th-16th century. The Jami Masjid with its stunning carved pillars and five domes, the stepwells, the Kalika Mata Temple atop Pavagadh Hill (accessible by ropeway), and the intricately carved Helical Stepwell showcase the cultural synthesis of Gujarat's golden age.

name: Bhuj
description: The historic city of the Kutch region, known for its vibrant handicrafts, the magnificent Aina Mahal (Palace of Mirrors), the Prag Mahal with its Italian Gothic architecture, and the 16th-century Bhujia Fort on the hilltop. The nearby Kutch Desert Festival, the Chhari Dhand wetland (flamingo sanctuary), and the traditional Bhujodi weavers village showcase Kutch's rich cultural heritage.

name: Junagadh
description: An ancient city at the foot of the Girnar Hills, famous for the 2,000-year-old Ashokan rock edicts (Brahmi script inscriptions), the magnificent Junagadh Fort (Uperkot), the intricately carved Mahabat Maqbara mausoleum, and the Girnar Jain Temples (accessible by climbing 10,000 steps). The Sakkarbaug Zoo and nearby Gir National Park are popular wildlife attractions.

name: Ambaji Temple
description: One of the 51 Shakti Peethas and one of the most important Goddess temples in India. The temple features no idol but a holy pith (shrine) with a light that represents the goddess Arasuri Amba. The nearby Gabbar Hill (with a 999-step climb), the Kailash Hill viewpoints, and the vibrant Navratri celebrations make it a major spiritual and cultural destination.

name: Shekhawati (Mandawa)
description: An open-air art gallery of Rajasthan, known for its exquisitely painted havelis from the 18th-19th century built by wealthy merchants. The frescoes depicting mythological scenes, British inventions (trains, cars, telephones), and daily life cover every inch of walls and ceilings. Mandawa, Nawalgarh, and Fatehpur have the most stunning examples of this unique Shekhawati art.

name: Osian (Osiyan)
description: An ancient temple town in the Thar Desert, known as the 'Khajuraho of Rajasthan' for its cluster of 9th-12th century Hindu and Jain temples. The Sachiya Mata Temple, Sun Temple, and Mahavira Jain Temple feature intricate carvings. Located on the historic Silk Route, the town also offers desert safaris and a glimpse into traditional Marwari culture.

name: Kozhikode (Calicut)
description: The historic port city where Vasco da Gama first landed in India in 1498. Known for the Kozhikode Beach, the 300-year-old Pazhassiraja Museum, the Kappad Beach (where da Gama landed), the Mananchira Square, and the Beypore port (famous for traditional dhow shipbuilding). Kozhikode is world-famous for its Malabar cuisine and especially the Kozhikode biryani.

name: Vagamon
description: A pristine hill station at 1,100 meters in the Western Ghats, surrounded by tea gardens, pine forests, and lush meadows. The Vagamon Hill (Thangal Para), Kurishumala Church, Paragliding point, and the Pine Forest are major attractions. Known for its perfect weather year-round and as one of the few places in Kerala where paragliding, trekking, and rock climbing are available.

name: Srikalahasti Temple
description: One of the Pancha Bhoota Stalas (representing the vayu/wind element), this 5th-century temple on the banks of the Swarnamukhi River is famous for its unique Vayulingam and the intricate 100-pillar mandapa with stunning Vijayanagara carvings. The Rahu-Ketu puja performed here is believed to remove astrological afflictions, drawing devotees from across India.

name: Ahobilam Temple
description: A sacred Vaishnavite pilgrimage site in the Nallamala Hills, famous for nine temples (Nava Narasimhas) dedicated to Lord Narasimha (the half-man half-lion avatar of Vishnu). The breathtaking setting amidst the Eastern Ghats, the waterfall-fed temple ponds, and the ancient Ahobilam Math make it one of the most spiritually significant and scenic pilgrimage destinations in South India.

name: Bhadrachalam Temple
description: A famous temple dedicated to Lord Rama, situated on the banks of the Godavari River. Built in the 17th century, the temple is renowned for its connection to the Bhakti poet-saint Ramadasu, who composed the Telugu opera 'Dasarathi Shatakamu' here. The annual Kalyanotsavam (celestial wedding of Rama and Sita) during Sri Rama Navami attracts hundreds of thousands of devotees.

name: Basara Saraswati Temple
description: One of the two major Saraswati (goddess of knowledge) temples in India, situated on the banks of the Godavari River. A major destination for students seeking blessings before exams, the temple was built in the 6th century by the Rajeshwara Mukuteshwara dynasty. The annual Vasanta Panchami festival with lakhs of devotees is a spectacular cultural event.

name: Murshidabad
description: The historic capital of Bengal under the Nawabs, famous for the majestic Hazarduari Palace (with 1,000 doors, now a museum), the Katra Masjid (a massive 18th-century mosque with underground tunnels), the Motijheel Lake, and the Khushbagh garden (the Nawab's family cemetery). The Murshidabad silk and brass work are traditional handicrafts of the region.

name: Digha Beach
description: The most popular beach destination in West Bengal, known for its 8-km long golden sand beach along the Bay of Bengal. The Marine Science Aquarium (one of the largest in India), Amravati Park, the Shankarpur fishing harbor, the Talsari Beach (with a sea shell museum), and the nearby Chandaneswar Shiva Temple make it a favorite weekend getaway for Kolkata residents.

name: Gorumara National Park
description: A lesser-known national park in the Dooars region, famous for the Indian one-horned rhinoceros population, along with elephants, bison, and leopards. The park's grasslands, riverine forests, and the Gorumara Watchtower (offering panoramic views) provide excellent wildlife viewing opportunities. Best visited in winter when migratory birds flock to the park.

name: Kesaria Stupa
description: The largest Buddhist stupa in the world, standing at 32 meters with a circumference of 400 meters. Built by Emperor Ashoka and the Licchavi kingdom, the brick stupa features impressive terraced architecture. It marks the site where Lord Buddha gave his last alms before entering Mahaparinirvana. An important but lesser-known Buddhist pilgrimage site of immense historic scale.

name: Jamshedpur
description: India's first planned industrial city, founded by Jamsetji Tata in 1907. The city is one of India's cleanest, known for the Jubilee Park (a 240-acre landscaped garden with illuminated fountains), the Tata Steel Zoological Park, the Dimna Lake (a scenic reservoir for water sports), and the Bhuvaneshwari Temple atop a hill. The Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary nearby houses elephants and tigers.

name: Gopalpur Beach
description: A serene, less-commercialized beach town on the Bay of Bengal, with a 10-km stretch of golden sand and casuarina groves. The historic Gopalpur port was once a bustling trading post used by the British. The calm waters, the nearby Rushikulya River mouth (where Olive Ridley turtles nest), and the Gopalpur lighthouse make it a peaceful coastal retreat.

name: Daringbadi
description: Called the 'Kashmir of Odisha', this hill station in the Eastern Ghats at 1,000 meters is famous for its coffee and pine groves, scenic valleys, and the Belghar waterfall. The Daringbadi Eco-Tourism Site, the Dhaba-style picnic spots, and the cool climate (occasional winter frost is rare for Odisha) make it a unique and emerging offbeat destination.

name: Mainpat
description: Known as the 'Mini Tibet of Chhattisgarh', a picturesque plateau at 1,100 meters in the Surguja district, home to a Tibetan refugee settlement with a beautiful Buddhist monastery. The Fish Point, Tiger Point, and the spectacular underground caves formed by volcanic activity offer breathtaking views of the surrounding valleys and dense forests.

name: Jorhat
description: The cultural capital of Assam and the tea capital of India, situated in the heart of the tea-growing belt. The Tocklai Tea Research Centre, the historic Thengal Manor (a 1920s tea planter's bungalow), the Majuli Island ferry access, the Kakochang Waterfall, and the Gibbon Wildlife Sanctuary (the only sanctuary named after a primate) make Jorhat a unique base for exploring Upper Assam tea gardens and wildlife.

name: Tezpur
description: A historic city on the banks of the Brahmaputra, known for its ancient ruins and scenic beauty. The Agnigarh Hill (a legendary site from the romance of Usha and Aniruddha), the Bamuni Hills with 9th-10th century sculptural remains, the Mahabhairav Temple, and the scenic Nameri National Park (famous for elephants and white-winged wood ducks) are key attractions. The city offers spectacular views of the Himalayas.

name: Patnitop
description: A beautiful meadow plateau at 2,024 meters on the Jammu-Srinagar highway, known for its lush green slopes, pine forests, and panoramic views of the Himalayan peaks. The Patnitop skywalk, paragliding over the valley, the Naag Mandir temple, and the Batote pine forest make it a popular stopover for travelers heading to the Kashmir Valley.

name: Yuksom
description: The historic first capital of Sikkim, where the first Chogyal (king) was consecrated in 1642. The Norbugang Coronation Throne, the Kathok Lake, and the 18th-century Dubdi Monastery are key historic sites. Yuksom is the starting point for treks to Mount Kanchenjunga base camp and the sacred Khecheopalri Lake, making it a premier destination for history buffs and trekkers alike.

name: Mecca Masjid
description: One of the largest mosques in India, built with bricks from Mecca, beside Charminar in the old city of Hyderabad.

name: Haji Ali Dargah
description: Iconic mosque and dargah on an islet in the Arabian Sea, connected by a narrow causeway that submerges at high tide.

name: Sidi Saiyyed Mosque
description: Famous for its exquisite jali (stone lattice) windows depicting the Tree of Life, a symbol of Ahmedabad.

name: Hazratbal Shrine
description: White marble mosque on the banks of Dal Lake, housing a relic believed to be a hair of Prophet Muhammad.

name: St. Thomas Cathedral
description: Mumbai's oldest British-era Anglican church, dating to 1718, near Flora Fountain.

name: San Thome Basilica
description: Roman Catholic basilica built over the tomb of St. Thomas the Apostle on the Marina Beach shore.

name: Christ Church Shimla
description: Second-oldest church in North India, a neo-Gothic landmark on The Ridge of Shimla.

name: St. Paul's Cathedral
description: Anglican cathedral in Indo-Gothic style, one of Kolkata's most photographed heritage churches.

name: Golden Temple (Harmandir Sahib)
description: Holiest Sikh shrine, gold-plated sanctum in the middle of the Amrit Sarovar sacred pool.

name: Gurudwara Bangla Sahib
description: Prominent Sikh house of worship known for its golden dome, sarovar, and free langar for all visitors.

name: Hemkund Sahib
description: High-altitude Sikh shrine at 4,329 m near a glacial lake, reached by a steep Himalayan trek.

name: Takht Sri Patna Sahib
description: One of the five Takhts of Sikhism, marking the birthplace of Guru Gobind Singh.

name: Takht Sri Kesgarh Sahib
description: Birthplace of the Khalsa, a major Sikh pilgrimage centre in the Shivalik foothills.

name: Chandni Chowk
description: Historic Mughal-era bazaar packed with street food, spices, jewellery, and wholesale lanes beside Red Fort.

name: Laad Bazaar
description: Colourful market near Charminar famous for lac bangles, pearls, and Hyderabadi wedding jewellery.

name: Crawford Market
description: Colonial-era covered market for fresh fruit, spices, and household goods in South Mumbai.

name: Devaraja Market
description: Traditional open-air market selling flowers, spices, textiles, and local Mysore specialities.

name: Tibetan Market McLeod Ganj
description: Bustling hillside market of Tibetan handicrafts, prayer flags, thangkas, and momos in Dharamshala.

name: Colva Beach Market
description: Lively beachside stalls selling Goan souvenirs, beachwear, and fresh seafood near Colva Beach.

name: Triund Trek
description: Classic Himalayan day trek above McLeod Ganj with sweeping views of the Dhauladhar range and camping at Triund top.

name: Valley of Flowers Trek
description: UNESCO World Heritage alpine meadow trek famed for monsoonal blooms between Govindghat and the valley.

name: Hampta Pass Trek
description: Dramatic crossover trek from lush Kullu valley to the barren high desert of Spiti via Hampta Pass.

name: Chadar Trek
description: Winter trek on the frozen Zanskar River — one of India's most iconic adventure routes.

name: Sandakphu Trek
description: Highest peak of West Bengal on the Singalila Ridge, with panoramic views of Everest, Kanchenjunga, and Lhotse.

name: Markha Valley Trek
description: Signature Ladakh trek through remote villages, high passes, and stark Himalayan landscapes.

name: Rishikesh White Water Rafting
description: India's rafting capital on the Ganges with grades from mellow stretches to thrilling Grade III–IV rapids.

name: Bir Billing Paragliding
description: World-renowned paragliding site hosting international competitions, with take-offs from Billing and landings in Bir.

name: Rann Adventure Camp
description: Desert adventure base near the White Rann with camel safaris, ATV rides, and full-moon salt-flat walks.

name: Havelock Scuba Diving
description: Crystal-clear Andaman waters with coral reefs, shipwrecks, and beginner to advanced dive sites around Havelock.

name: Jim Corbett Jeep Safari
description: Wildlife jeep safari in India's oldest national park, famous for Bengal tigers and rich birdlife.

name: Spiti Valley Bike Expedition
description: High-altitude motorcycle adventure across Kunzum Pass into the cold-desert villages of Spiti.

name: Dashashwamedh Ghat
description: Most celebrated ghat on the Ganges, famous for the nightly Ganga Aarti ceremony.

name: Assi Ghat
description: Southernmost main ghat of Varanasi, popular for morning yoga, boat rides, and quieter aarti.

name: Har Ki Pauri
description: Sacred bathing ghat on the Ganges where the evening Ganga Aarti draws throngs of pilgrims.

name: Ahilya Ghat
description: Stone ghats of the Narmada below Ahilya Fort, lined with temples and famous for sunset views.

name: Meenakshi Amman Temple
description: Dravidian temple complex with towering gopurams dedicated to Goddess Meenakshi and Lord Sundareswarar.

name: Brihadeeswara Temple
description: UNESCO Chola temple with a towering vimana, one of the greatest architectural marvels of South India.

name: Naini Lake
description: Kidney-shaped hill lake that gives Nainital its name, ringed by mall road and forested hills.

name: Cubbon Park
description: Historic lung of Bengaluru with tree-lined avenues, museums, and open lawns in the city centre.

name: Palolem Beach
description: Crescent-shaped south Goa beach known for calm waters, palm-fringed sand, and sunset canoe rides.

name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Vastu Sangrahalaya
description: Premier museum of art and archaeology in a Grade-I heritage Indo-Saracenic building facing the Arabian Sea.

name: Indian Museum Kolkata
description: Oldest and largest multipurpose museum in India, founded in 1814 with vast archaeological collections.

name: Bee Falls (Pachmarhi)
description: The tallest waterfall in Pachmarhi (~30 m), named for the bees that nest in nearby cliffs. A short forest walk leads to a misty plunge pool — a monsoon favourite of the Satpura hills.

name: Duchess Falls
description: A three-tiered cascade deep in the Pachmarhi forest, reached by a longer trek than Bee Falls. Quieter pools and dense greenery make it one of the Satpuras’ true hidden waterfalls.

name: Dhoopgarh Peak
description: Highest point of the Satpura Range (1,352 m). Famous for golden sunsets over endless forest ridges — the classic Pachmarhi viewpoint.

name: Jata Shankar Cave Temple
description: A natural cave shrine dedicated to Lord Shiva, believed to be where Shiva hid from the demon Bhasmasur. Cool subterranean chambers and a natural spring add to the pilgrimage feel.

name: Pandav Caves (Pachmarhi)
description: Five rock-cut Buddhist caves from the Gupta period, locally linked to the Pandavas’ exile. Compact heritage site with forest views above the town.

name: Handi Khoh
description: A dramatic 300-foot deep gorge near Pachmarhi, shrouded in mist and legend. Viewpoints overlook sheer cliffs and dense jungle — best after rains.

name: Rajat Prapat (Silver Falls)
description: Also called Big Fall, this silver stream of water drops from a high cliff near Apsara Vihar. A quieter alternative to Bee Falls with a short forest approach.

name: Chauragarh Temple
description: Hilltop Shiva shrine reached by climbing over a thousand stone steps lined with tridents offered by devotees. Panoramic Satpura views reward the trek, especially during Mahashivratri.

name: Jahaz Mahal (Ship Palace)
description: Iconic Mandu palace that appears to float between two lakes like a ship. Built by Ghiyas-ud-din Khilji in the 15th century; a masterpiece of Afghan-era architecture.

name: Rani Roopmati Pavilion
description: Hilltop pavilion linked to the legend of Baz Bahadur and Rani Roopmati, overlooking the Narmada valley. Sunset light on the sandstone arches is unforgettable.

name: Hindola Mahal
description: The “Swinging Palace” of Mandu — a massive audience hall with sloping walls that create an illusion of rocking. Stark, powerful Afghan architecture.

name: Jami Masjid Mandu
description: One of India’s finest Afghan mosques, modelled on the Great Mosque of Damascus. Vast courtyard, rows of arches, and serene proportions on Mandu’s plateau.

name: Baz Bahadur Palace
description: Palace of the last independent ruler of Mandu, set below Roopmati Pavilion. Courtyards, reservoirs, and valley views recall the famous love story of Baz Bahadur and Roopmati.

name: Orchha Chhatris (Cenotaphs)
description: Fourteen riverside cenotaphs of Bundela kings along the Betwa. One of India’s most photogenic sunset spots — spires reflected in the river at golden hour.

name: Chaturbhuj Temple Orchha
description: Towering multi-storeyed temple built for an idol of Rama that never arrived (the idol stayed at Ram Raja Temple). Climb for rooftop views over Orchha’s palaces and river.

name: Lakshmi Narayan Temple Orchha
description: Fort-like temple on a ridge with rare Bundela murals — battle scenes, myths, and everyday life painted on walls and ceilings. Often quieter than the fort complex.

name: Jahangir Mahal Orchha
description: Grand Bundela palace built to welcome Emperor Jahangir. Domed chhatris, courtyards, and blue-tiled accents make it the centrepiece of Orchha Fort.

name: Sas Bahu Temples
description: Pair of elaborately carved 11th-century Vishnu temples inside Gwalior Fort. “Sas-Bahu” means mother-in-law and daughter-in-law — dense sculpture and soaring shikharas.

name: Teli ka Mandir
description: Unusual Dravidian-meets-North-Indian temple inside Gwalior Fort, standing ~30 m tall. One of the oldest surviving structures on the fort hill.

name: Gujari Mahal (Archaeological Museum)
description: 15th-century palace built by Raja Man Singh Tomar for Queen Mrignayani, now an archaeological museum. Famous for the rare Salabhanjika (Woman with Tree) sculpture.

name: Tansen Tomb
description: Tomb of the legendary musician Tansen near the tomb of his Sufi guru Mohammad Ghaus. A quiet heritage corner; site of the annual Tansen Music Festival.

name: Jai Vilas Palace
description: Opulent Scindia palace (1874) housing the Jiwaji Rao Scindia Museum. European ballrooms, crystal furniture, and the famous double-storey Durbar Hall chandelier.

name: Sun Temple Gwalior
description: Modern temple inspired by Konark’s Sun Temple, set in landscaped gardens. Popular local pilgrimage and photo spot away from the fort crowds.

name: Kal Bhairav Temple
description: Ancient temple to Kal Bhairav on the Shipra’s bank. Unique ritual of offering liquor to the deity; one of Ujjain’s most powerful Shakti–Shaiva sites.

name: Harsiddhi Temple
description: One of the 51 Shakti Peethas, famous for its towering lamp pillars lit during Navratri. Centrally located near Mahakaleshwar.

name: Ram Ghat Ujjain
description: Principal ghat on the Shipra River, centre of the Simhastha Kumbh Mela. Evening aarti and lamp offerings create a deeply spiritual riverside atmosphere.

name: Kaliadeh Palace
description: Island palace on the Shipra built by the Sultans of Mandu and later restored by the Scindias. Persian inscriptions and river islets feel far from city bustle.

name: Vedh Shala (Jantar Mantar Ujjain)
description: Observatory built by Maharaja Jai Singh II, similar in spirit to Jaipur’s Jantar Mantar. Ancient instruments for measuring time and planetary positions.

name: Sandipani Ashram
description: Legendary ashram where Lord Krishna and Sudama studied under Guru Sandipani. Quiet temple complex with the Gomti Kund water tank.

name: Raneh Falls
description: Canyon waterfall on the Ken River carved through ancient volcanic rock in shades of pink, grey, and yellow. A spectacular monsoon outing near Khajuraho and Panna.

name: Pandav Falls
description: Scenic waterfall and picnic spot inside Panna Tiger Reserve area, with caves linked to Pandava legends. Combine with a Ken River drive.

name: Kandariya Mahadeva Temple
description: Largest and most ornate temple of Khajuraho’s Western Group — peak of Chandela Nagara architecture with thousands of sculptures and a towering shikhara.

name: Eastern Group Temples Khajuraho
description: Cluster of Jain and Hindu temples east of the main Western Group, including Parsvanath and Adinath. Quieter, richly carved, and often overlooked by day-trippers.

name: Ajaigarh Fort
description: Hill fort of the Chandela era near Panna, with temples, gates, and sweeping views over the Ken valley. A rugged, less-visited Bundelkhand stronghold.

name: Kalinjar Fort
description: Legendary hill fort on the Vindhyas, fought over by Chandelas, Mughals, and British. Rock-cut shrines, Neelkanth temple, and massive ramparts reward a full day.

name: Bateshwar Temple Complex
description: Vast complex of nearly 200 ruined and restored temples near Padavali, rediscovered and conserved in recent decades. One of India’s great “lost temple city” experiences.

name: Mitawali Chausath Yogini Temple
description: Circular 64-Yogini temple on a hill near Padavali, often compared to India’s Parliament building in plan. Atmospheric ruins with panoramic countryside views.

name: Padavali Garhi Temple
description: Exquisitely carved temple inside a fort-like enclosure near Bateshwar. Dense sculpture and a fortress setting make it a hidden Bundelkhand gem.

name: Datia Palace (Bir Singh Deo Palace)
description: Seven-storeyed Bundela palace built by Raja Bir Singh Deo, never fully occupied by royalty. Labyrinthine corridors and rooftop views over Datia town.

name: Sonagiri Jain Temples
description: Hill of white-spired Digambar Jain temples — over 70 shrines on a sacred ridge between Datia and Gwalior. A luminous pilgrimage landscape at sunrise.

name: Chanderi Fort
description: Historic hill fort overlooking the weaving town famous for Chanderi sarees. Gates, palaces, and valley views from a quieter heritage destination.

name: Kati Ghati Chanderi
description: Dramatic gateway cut through solid rock — legend says it was carved in a single night. A signature photo stop on the approach to Chanderi.

name: Badal Mahal Gate Chanderi
description: Ornate ceremonial gate of Chanderi with Indo-Islamic arches. Remnant of the town’s medieval court culture and silk-trading prosperity.

name: Madhav National Park
description: Former hunting grounds of the Scindias around Sakhya and Madhav Sagar lakes. Safaris for deer, birds, and occasional leopards; George Castle viewpoint nearby.

name: Scindia Chhatris Shivpuri
description: Marble cenotaphs of Scindia rulers set in formal Mughal-style gardens. Intricate inlay work and peaceful lawns in the heart of Shivpuri.

name: Narwar Fort
description: Massive hill fort associated with the legendary Raja Nal, sprawling across ridges near Shivpuri. Ruined palaces and long fortification walls for serious explorers.

name: Chachai Falls
description: One of India’s tallest waterfalls (~130 m) on the Bihad River near Rewa. A roaring monsoon spectacle still relatively unknown outside the region.

name: Keoti Falls
description: Powerful waterfall on the Mahana River near Rewa, plunging into a rocky gorge. Best visited in monsoon and early winter when flow is strong.

name: Bahuti Falls
description: Tall cascade on the Odda River in Rewa district — among Madhya Pradesh’s highest falls. A remote nature destination for waterfall hunters.

name: White Tiger Safari Mukundpur
description: Safari park near Rewa dedicated to white tigers — Rewa is the historic home of the white tiger lineage. Combine with nearby Govindgarh and Chachai Falls.

name: Govindgarh Palace Rewa
description: Lakeside palace of the Rewa royals, linked to the story of the first white tiger kept in captivity. Serene reservoir setting in eastern Madhya Pradesh.

name: Kuno National Park
description: Habitat of India’s Project Cheetah reintroduction. Dry deciduous forests and grasslands of the Kuno River valley — a landmark wildlife destination of modern India.

name: Sanjay Dubri National Park
description: Tiger reserve on the MP–Chhattisgarh border with sal forests and the Banas River. Quiet alternative to famous parks like Kanha and Bandhavgarh.

name: Ghughua Fossil National Park
description: Unique park protecting fossilised plants from ~60 million years ago — petrified trees and leaves in situ. A rare palaeobotany destination in Mandla–Dindori belt.

name: Udayagiri Caves
description: Gupta-era rock-cut caves famous for the monumental Varaha (boar avatar of Vishnu) relief. Essential ancient-Indian art stop near Sanchi.

name: Heliodorus Pillar (Khamba Baba)
description: 2nd-century BCE Garuda pillar erected by Indo-Greek ambassador Heliodorus — early evidence of Vaishnava devotion. A quiet, historically crucial site near Vidisha.

name: Raisen Fort
description: Hilltop fort east of Bhopal with palaces, temples, and a large water tank. Wide views over the Malwa plateau; lightly touristed weekend escape.

name: Islamnagar Fort
description: Former capital of the Bhopal Nawabs before the move to Bhopal city. Chaman Mahal and Rani Mahal gardens survive in a quiet village setting.

name: Lower Lake (Chhota Talaab)
description: Companion lake to Upper Lake (Bhojtal), linked by a bridge and promenades. Evening walks, boating, and city reflections in the heart of Bhopal.

name: Birla Mandir Bhopal (Lakshmi Narayan)
description: White marble Lakshmi Narayan temple on Arera Hills with city panoramas. Peaceful complex popular with families and evening visitors.

name: Shaukat Mahal
description: Indo-European palace near Bhopal’s Chowk, blending Gothic and Islamic motifs. Part of the old Qila area with Sadar Manzil nearby.

name: Bhimkund Bajna
description: Natural deep kund associated with Mahabharata legends, with crystal-clear water in a rocky cleft near Chhatarpur–Bajna. A serene offbeat swim-and-picnic spot (local rules apply).

name: Bijasen Tekri
description: Hilltop Bijasen Mata temple with panoramic views over Indore. Popular sunset point and quiet alternative to city temples.

name: Pipliyapala Regional Park
description: Large urban lake park with walking trails, musical fountain, and boating. Favourite evening leisure spot for Indore locals.

name: Choral Dam
description: Scenic reservoir in the hills south of Indore, popular for monsoon drives, photography, and quiet lakeside picnics near Tincha Falls belt.

name: Bagh Caves
description: Rock-cut Buddhist caves (5th–6th century) once famous for murals comparable to Ajanta. Remote Dhar district gem for art and archaeology lovers.

name: Asirgarh Fort
description: Strategic “key to the Deccan” fort controlling the Burhanpur pass. Massive walls, mosques, and cliff views — a powerful, under-visited fortress.

name: Shahi Qila Burhanpur
description: Riverside Mughal fort on the Tapti where Mumtaz Mahal once lived; local lore links Burhanpur to early plans for the Taj Mahal. Quiet riverside heritage.

name: Dhar Fort (Fort of Dhar)
description: Historic fort of the Paramaras and later Muslim rulers of Malwa, near Mandu. Red sandstone walls and city views from a less-crowded heritage town.

name: Ahilya Fort Maheshwar
description: Fort-palace of Rani Ahilyabai Holkar above the Narmada ghats. Courtyards, temples, and river panoramas — the heart of Maheshwar’s heritage identity.

name: Mamleshwar Temple
description: Jyotirlinga shrine on the south bank of the Narmada, traditionally paired with Omkareshwar on the island. Completes the Omkareshwar pilgrimage circuit.

name: Narmada Udgam Temple
description: Sacred source temple of the Narmada River in the Maikal Hills. Pilgrims begin the Narmada Parikrama here amid forests and ancient shrines.

name: Kapildhara Falls
description: Waterfall on the infant Narmada a short trek from Amarkantak town. Forest trail and plunge pool named after sage Kapil — peaceful pilgrimage nature spot.

name: Sonmuda Viewpoint
description: Origin viewpoint of the Son River near Amarkantak. Plateau forests and monsoon mist — a quieter companion to Narmada Udgam.

name: Hanuwantiya (Indira Sagar)
description: Island tourism hub on Indira Sagar reservoir with Jal Mahotsav events, water sports, and hill camping. A modern hidden holiday pocket of Nimar.

name: Ramghat Chitrakoot
description: Sacred ghat on the Mandakini River where evening aarti draws throngs of pilgrims. Spiritual heart of Chitrakoot’s Ramayana landscape.

name: Kamadgiri Hill
description: Forest hill considered the spiritual core of Chitrakoot; devotees complete a 5 km parikrama past temples and caves linked to Rama’s exile.

name: Gupta Godavari Caves
description: Pair of caves with a perennial stream said to be a hidden Godavari. Cool, mystical stop on the Chitrakoot pilgrimage circuit.

name: Sphatik Shila
description: Two rocks on the Mandakini where Rama and Sita are believed to have rested. Footprints and forest river setting — intimate Ramayana lore site.

name: Sahastradhara Mandla
description: Where the Narmada splits into countless streams over rocky beds near Mandla. Sacred bathing spot and photogenic shallow cascades.

name: Rani Durgavati Samadhi Mandla
description: Memorial to the Gond queen Rani Durgavati near Mandla. Quiet historic site honouring her resistance against Mughal forces.

name: Marble Rocks Viewpoint Bhedaghat
description: Cliff viewpoints above the Narmada’s white marble gorge — complementary to the boat ride through Marble Rocks. Best light in late afternoon.

name: Bhedaghat Cable Car
description: Ropeway soaring over the Narmada marble gorge and Dhuandhar mist. Short, spectacular ride linking viewpoints above the falls.

name: Tripuri (Tewar) Heritage Site
description: Ancient capital of the Kalachuri dynasty near Jabalpur, with temple ruins and archaeological remains. Offbeat history stop for heritage travellers.

name: Narsinghgarh Fort and Lake
description: Picturesque fort above a lake in Rajgarh district — often called a mini-Switzerland of MP for its green hills and water views. Quiet Malwa getaway.

name: Tawa Reservoir
description: Vast reservoir on the Tawa River adjoining Satpura Tiger Reserve. Boating, islands, and monsoon landscapes on the approach to Pachmarhi.

name: Orchha Wildlife Sanctuary
description: Compact sanctuary along the Betwa with walking trails, deer, and birdlife — pair heritage Orchha with a short nature walk.

name: Ken Gharial Sanctuary
description: Sanctuary protecting gharials and other wildlife along the Ken River near Panna. Dramatic gorges and riverine habitat for nature lovers.

name: Gandhi Sagar Sanctuary
description: Wildlife sanctuary around Gandhi Sagar reservoir on the Chambal. Cliffs, waterbirds, and quiet landscapes near Mandsaur’s Pashupatinath circuit.

name: Raja Mahal Orchha
description: Older Bundela palace within Orchha Fort with murals and courtyard architecture. Less ornate than Jahangir Mahal but rich in early Bundela history.

name: Sanchi Archaeological Museum
description: ASI museum beside the Great Stupa displaying gateways, relics, and sculptures from Sanchi’s Buddhist complex. Essential context for the UNESCO site.

name: Sanchi Stupa No. 2
description: Older hilltop stupa west of the Great Stupa, with finely carved railings. Quieter corner of the Sanchi complex often skipped by hurried visitors.

name: Bhojpur Rock Quarry
description: Ancient unfinished quarry near Bhojeshwar Temple showing how massive stone blocks were cut for the Shiva lingam temple. Fascinating engineering archaeology.

name: Halali Dam (Samrat Ashok Sagar)
description: Large reservoir north of Bhopal popular for weekend nature drives, birding, and monsoon landscapes. Peaceful escape from city noise.

name: Phen Wildlife Sanctuary
description: Buffer wilderness near Kanha Tiger Reserve with similar sal–bamboo habitat. Quieter forest experience for those exploring Mandla’s wild belt.

name: Singhori Wildlife Sanctuary
description: Forest sanctuary in Raisen district protecting dry deciduous wildlife corridors. Offbeat nature stop between Bhopal and central MP.

name: Jamnera Hills / Eco Tourism
description: Hilly eco-tourism pocket near Jabalpur with viewpoints and forest walks — a local weekend nature escape beyond Dumna and Bargi.

name: Bandhavgarh Fort
description: Ancient fort on a cliff inside Bandhavgarh Tiger Reserve, linked to legends of Rama and Lakshmana. Restricted access via park rules — iconic skyline of the reserve.

name: Maihar Fort
description: Hill fort above Maihar town, often combined with the Sharda Devi temple climb. Views over the Satna–Maihar countryside.

name: Muktagiri Jain Temples
description: Cluster of Digambar Jain temples in the Satpura foothills near Betul–Amravati border. Waterfalls, forest steps, and white shrines — a serene pilgrimage trek.

name: Jogi Bhadak Waterfall
description: Lesser-known cascade near the Tincha–Patalpani belt outside Indore. A monsoon trek destination for locals seeking quieter falls.

name: Triveni Museum Ujjain
description: Museum showcasing the culture and history of the Malwa–Ujjain region, complementary to Mahakaleshwar and Shipra ghats.

name: Chaturbhuj Temple Khajuraho
description: Southern Group temple with a large ornate Vishnu image and refined Chandela carving. Quieter than the Western Group yet richly sculpted.

name: Duladeo Temple Khajuraho
description: Late Chandela Shiva temple of the Southern Group with graceful apsara sculptures. Often missed by visitors focused only on Kandariya Mahadeva.

name: Totladoh Dam Pench
description: Large reservoir on the Pench River forming the park aquatic edge. Scenic drives and birdlife on the Madhya Pradesh side of Pench.

name: Kanha Mukki Gate Zone
description: Quieter safari zone of Kanha Tiger Reserve accessed via Mukki gate. Meadows and sal forest with fewer vehicles than Kisli on busy days.

name: Pashupatinath Ghats Mandsaur
description: Riverfront ghats beside the unique eight-faced Pashupatinath lingam temple on the Shivna River. Spiritual twin experience to the main shrine.

name: Sukhanandji Ashram Waterfall
description: Ashram and waterfall retreat in Neemuch district — a peaceful Malwa nature-spiritual stop away from major tourist circuits.

name: Chaturbhujnath Nala Rock Art
description: Open-air rock-art galleries in Mandsaur–Bhanpura region with prehistoric paintings along Chaturbhujnath Nala. A true archaeological hidden gem of Malwa.

name: Nidan Falls (Katangi Waterfall)
description: Also called Katangi Waterfall / Nidan Kund, about 40 km from Jabalpur. Twin cascades drop over 100 feet between dark step-like rocks; best in monsoon with a short trek from the main road.

name: Paat Baba Mandir (Pat Baba Hanuman)
description: Historic Hanuman temple inside the Gun Carriage Factory estate, established 12 August 1903 by British officer Stanley Smith after a buried Hanuman idol was unearthed. Famous Tuesday aarti; deeply revered in Jabalpur.

name: Hanumantal Bada Jain Mandir
description: Large Digambar Jain temple complex around Hanumantal tank in old Jabalpur — ornate shrines and a major local pilgrimage landmark.

name: Kachnar City Lord Shiva Statue
description: Tall sitting Shiva statue at Kachnar City temple complex — popular photo and devotion stop in Jabalpur (related to Kachnar City Shiva Temple).

name: Rahatgarh Waterfalls
description: Multi-tier waterfall near Sagar–Rahatgarh, strongest in monsoon. Popular local picnic and photography spot.

name: Purwa Falls
description: Powerful Rewa-region waterfall on the plateau circuit with Chachai and Keoti. Raw monsoon force and village landscapes.

name: Indira Gandhi Rashtriya Manav Sangrahalaya
description: National Museum of Mankind on Shamla Hills — open-air tribal habitats, ethnographic galleries, and lakeside trails.

name: Madhya Pradesh Tribal Museum
description: Award-winning museum of MP tribal cultures with immersive installations and crafts — a modern cultural highlight of Bhopal.

name: Rewa Fort
description: Historic seat of the Rewa royals with museum galleries and White Tiger heritage. Anchor site for the Rewa waterfall circuit.

name: Deur Kothar Stupas
description: Mauryan-era Buddhist stupa complex near Rewa — among India’s earliest Buddhist remains and a true archaeological gem.

name: Ahukhana Burhanpur
description: Mughal garden-tomb complex where Mumtaz Mahal was temporarily buried before the Taj Mahal. Quiet riverside heritage of Burhanpur.

name: Man Singh Palace (Gwalior Fort)
description: Iconic blue-tiled palace of Raja Man Singh Tomar on Gwalior Fort — geometric glaze work and underground chambers.

name: Ratapani Wildlife Sanctuary
description: Large forest tract near Bhopal–Raisen with tiger habitat significance. Quiet wilderness day-trip alternative to city parks.

name: Nauradehi Wildlife Sanctuary
description: One of MP’s largest sanctuaries across Sagar–Damoh–Narsinghpur — expansive forests and landscape conservation importance.

name: Parsvanath Temple Khajuraho
description: Largest Jain temple of Khajuraho’s Eastern Group with exquisite exterior carving — beyond the famous Western Group.

name: Kanha Canopy Walk Mukki
description: Tree-top canopy walk near Mukki gate of Kanha — elevated forest views as a safari trip add-on.

name: Hampi (Virupaksha Temple)
description: Living temple at the heart of the UNESCO Hampi ruins — Vijayanagara capital landscape of boulder hills and monuments.

name: Living Root Bridges (Nongriat)
description: Famous double-decker living root bridge grown from rubber-tree roots — iconic Meghalaya jungle trek.

name: Pangong Tso
description: High-altitude brackish lake changing shades of blue — legendary Ladakh landscape beyond Leh.

name: White Rann of Kutch
description: Vast salt desert famous for Rann Utsav full-moon nights, crafts, and endless white landscapes.

name: Alappuzha Backwaters
description: Classic Kerala houseboat waterways of canals, lagoons, and paddy fields — the Venice of the East.

name: Tiger Hill Darjeeling
description: Famous sunrise viewpoint over Kanchenjunga from the Queen of the Hills.

name: Mohadi Waterfall
description: Seasonal cascade in the Dewas–Indore belt, lively after rains. A quieter alternative to Tincha and Patalpani for weekend nature trips.

name: Apsara Vihar
description: Small natural pool and cascade near Rajat Prapat in Pachmarhi. Legend links it to apsaras bathing — a short, scenic forest stop.

name: Kakda Kho Mandu
description: Deep ravine viewpoint on Mandu’s plateau edge. Dramatic cliffs and valley drop — a lesser-visited nature corner beyond the palace circuit.

name: Central Museum Indore
description: Indore’s main archaeological and art museum with sculptures, coins, and Holkar-era artefacts. Compact heritage stop near the city centre.

name: Khajuraho Archaeological Museum
description: ASI museum near the Western Group displaying sculptures and architectural fragments from Khajuraho temples. Essential context for the UNESCO site.

name: Bison Lodge Interpretation Centre
description: Colonial-era lodge turned Satpura interpretation centre covering wildlife, geology, and Pachmarhi’s forest heritage.

name: Bandhavgarh Museum
description: Small park interpretation museum near Bandhavgarh with exhibits on tigers, flora, and the reserve’s history — good pre-safari stop.

name: Ashrafi Mahal Mandu
description: Ruined madrasa and victory tower complex opposite Jami Masjid. Once among Mandu’s grandest ensembles; atmospheric Afghan-era remains.

name: Koshak Mahal Chanderi
description: Unfinished multi-storey palace of Chanderi’s medieval rulers. Massive stone floors and arches overlooking the weaving town.

name: Budhi Chanderi
description: Older ruined settlement near modern Chanderi with fort remains and temples. Atmospheric “old Chanderi” for heritage walkers.

name: Bijawar Fort
description: Hill fort in Chhatarpur district on the Bundelkhand circuit near Khajuraho–Panna. Quiet ramparts and small-town heritage.

name: Assi Khamba Ki Bawdi
description: Historic stepwell with eighty pillars inside Gwalior Fort precincts. Cool stone architecture often missed between the main temples.

name: Pravin Raj Mahal Orchha
description: Palace linked to the poetess-courtesan Rai Praveen within Orchha’s fort complex. Intimate courtyards and Bundela detailing.

name: Adinath Temple Khajuraho
description: Eastern Group Jain temple with refined sculpture, quieter than the Western Group. Part of Khajuraho’s important Jain cluster.

name: Bhairav Baba Temple Khamdeeh
description: Unusual shrine with a massive reclining Bhairav idol near Rewa. Striking spiritual stop on the eastern MP waterfall route.

name: Achleshwar Mandir
description: Historic Shiva temple in Gwalior associated with local legends of the fort’s founding. Active neighbourhood pilgrimage site.

name: Jamuni Sangam Orchha
description: Confluence point near Orchha’s Betwa riverfront — peaceful viewpoint for chhatri silhouettes and evening light.

name: Alamgiri Gate Mandu
description: Monumental gateway on Mandu’s fortifications, associated with later Islamic rulers. Strong photo stop on the plateau approach.

name: Chaman Mahal Islamnagar
description: Garden palace of the early Bhopal Nawabs at Islamnagar, with charbagh-style layout. Pair with nearby Rani Mahal and fort walls.

name: Sardarpur Wildlife Sanctuary
description: Lesser-known sanctuary in Dhar district protecting grassland and woodland species, including the rare kharmor (lesser florican) habitat story of western MP.

name: Dinosaur Fossil National Park (Dhar/Bagh belt)
description: Fossil-rich landscape of western MP associated with dinosaur eggs and Cretaceous finds near the Bagh–Dhar region. Niche geo-tourism destination.

name: Shipra River Ghats Ujjain
description: Stretch of sacred Shipra ghats beyond Ram Ghat — evening lamps, pilgrims, and Simhastha heritage along Ujjain’s holy riverfront.

name: Rani Talab Rewa
description: Historic tank in Rewa surrounded by temples and chhatris. Calm city heritage stop before heading to Chachai or Keoti Falls.

name: Maihar Hill (Sharda Devi Climb)
description: Stepped hill climb to Sharda Devi temple — ropeway optional. Spiritual trek with wide views over Maihar and the Satna countryside.

name: Solang Valley
description: Adventure valley near Manali for paragliding, ropeway and snow sports with Himalayan views.

name: Key Monastery
description: Hilltop Tibetan Buddhist monastery overlooking Spiti River — iconic high-altitude monastery of Spiti.

name: Lakshman Jhula
description: Iconic suspension bridge over the Ganga — yoga capital landmark with riverside temples and cafes.

name: Bhangarh Fort
description: Ruined fort often called India’s most haunted — atmospheric heritage site near Alwar.

name: Shirdi Sai Baba Temple
description: Major pilgrimage temple of Sai Baba — one of India’s most visited spiritual destinations.

name: Bhaja Caves
description: Ancient Buddhist rock-cut caves near Lonavala — quieter alternative to Karla Caves.

name: Arthur’s Seat Mahabaleshwar
description: Dramatic cliff viewpoint over the Krishna valley — classic Mahabaleshwar sunset spot.

name: Abbey Falls
description: Scenic waterfall amid coffee plantations near Madikeri — classic Coorg nature stop.

name: Om Beach Gokarna
description: Om-shaped beach cove — quieter coastal alternative to Goa with temple town vibe.

name: Padmanabhaswamy Temple
description: Grand Vishnu temple of Travancore — Dravidian architecture and legendary treasure vaults.

name: Chinese Fishing Nets Fort Kochi
description: Iconic cantilever fishing nets on Fort Kochi’s waterfront — colonial heritage quarter.

name: Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary
description: Tiger reserve around Periyar Lake — boat safaris for elephants and birds in the Western Ghats.

name: Shore Temple Mahabalipuram
description: 7th-century Pallava shore temple on the Bay of Bengal — UNESCO Group of Monuments.

name: Government Botanical Garden Ooty
description: Historic hillside botanical garden in the Nilgiris — flowers, lawns and fossil tree.

name: Tirumala Venkateswara Temple
description: One of the world’s richest and most visited temples — Lord Venkateswara on Tirumala hills.

name: Chandrabhaga Beach
description: Quiet beach near Konark Sun Temple — sunrise walks along the Odisha coast.

name: Jagannath Temple Puri
description: Char Dham temple of Lord Jagannath — famous Rath Yatra chariot festival.

name: Nalanda Mahavihara
description: UNESCO ruins of the ancient Buddhist university — stupas, monasteries and museums.

name: Khajjiar Lake
description: Meadow-lake often called Mini Switzerland of India — near Dalhousie in the Chamba hills.

name: Bharatpur Beach Neil Island
description: Shallow coral beach on Shaheed Dweep (Neil) — snorkelling and relaxed Andaman vibes.

name: Barnawapara Wildlife Sanctuary
description: Forest sanctuary with deer, leopards and tribal villages — quieter Central India wildlife.

name: Rock Garden Ranchi
description: Hillside rock garden and lake park — family leisure spot in Ranchi.

name: Humayun’s Tomb
description: UNESCO precursor to the Taj Mahal — Mughal garden-tomb complex in Delhi.

name: Tungnath Temple
description: Highest Shiva temple in the world — short trek from Chopta toward Chandrashila.

name: Sandakphu
description: Highest peak of West Bengal — trek for Sleeping Buddha view of Kanchenjunga.

name: Chandratal Lake
description: High-altitude crescent moon lake on the Spiti–Lahaul route — alpine camping gem.

name: Coaker’s Walk Kodaikanal
description: Paved cliff-edge promenade with misty valley views — classic Kodaikanal walk.

name: Promenade Beach Pondicherry
description: French Quarter seaside boulevard — colonial houses, Gandhi statue and Bay of Bengal breeze.

name: Auroville Matrimandir
description: Golden geodesic meditation sphere at the heart of the international township Auroville.

name: Kaziranga Elephant Safari Zone
description: Classic elephant and jeep safari zones of Kaziranga for one-horned rhinos and wild buffalo.

name: Dawki Umngot River
description: Crystal-clear river on the Bangladesh border — famous glass-bottom boat rides.

name: Naga Heritage Village Kisama
description: Venue of the Hornbill Festival showcasing Naga tribes, crafts and warrior traditions.

name: Neermahal Palace
description: Water palace of the Tripura kings on Rudrasagar Lake — unique Indo-Islamic architecture.

name: Tripura Sundari Temple
description: One of the 51 Shakti Peethas — major pilgrimage temple of Tripura.

name: INA Memorial Moirang
description: Memorial where Netaji’s INA flag was first hoisted on Indian soil in 1944.

name: Dubdi Monastery Yuksom
description: Oldest monastery of Sikkim in forested hills above Yuksom — quiet pilgrimage trek.

name: Magnetic Hill Ladakh
description: Optical-gravity illusion spot on Leh–Kargil highway where vehicles appear to roll uphill.

name: Gulmarg Gondola
description: One of Asia’s highest cable cars to Apharwat Peak — skiing and alpine meadows.

name: Betaab Valley
description: Film-famous green valley on Lidder River near Pahalgam — meadows and snow peaks.

name: Qila Mubarak Patiala
description: 18th-century Sikh fort-palace complex with murals and museums in Patiala.

name: Pinjore Gardens (Yadavindra)
description: Mughal-style terraced gardens near Chandigarh — fountains and monsoon palace.

name: Ranakpur Jain Temple
description: Marble Jain temple with 1,444 uniquely carved pillars in the Aravalli hills.

name: Nakki Lake Mount Abu
description: Sacred lake in Rajasthan’s only hill station — boating and sunset viewpoints.

name: Ajanta Cave 1
description: Highlight cave of Ajanta with Bodhisattva Padmapani mural — peak of ancient Indian painting.

name: Kailasa Temple Ellora
description: Monolithic rock-cut temple carved from a single cliff — Cave 16 of Ellora UNESCO site.

name: Sinhagad Fort
description: Historic hill fort near Pune associated with Tanaji Malusare — popular trek and monsoon views.

name: Kolaba Fort Alibaug
description: Sea fort reachable at low tide from Alibaug beach — Maratha coastal heritage.

name: Vitthala Temple Hampi
description: UNESCO Hampi highlight with stone chariot and musical pillars of Vijayanagara.

name: Chennakeshava Temple Belur
description: Hoysala star-shaped temple with intricate soapstone sculpture — UNESCO tentative site.

name: Hoysaleswara Temple Halebidu
description: Twin Hoysala temples with dense friezes — peak of medieval Karnataka art.

name: Badami Cave Temples
description: Rock-cut Chalukya caves around Agastya lake — early Deccan temple architecture.

name: Kanyakumari Vivekananda Rock
description: Memorial rock at India’s southern tip — sunrise/sunset over confluence of three seas.

name: Thanjavur Maratha Palace
description: Palace complex with Saraswathi Mahal Library near Brihadeeswarar Temple.

name: Kanadukathan Chettinad Mansions
description: Opulent Chettiar mansions with teak, tiles and courtyards — heritage food and architecture.

name: Thousand Pillar Temple
description: Kakatiya star-shaped temple of Rudreshwara — UNESCO World Heritage component.

name: Ramappa Temple
description: UNESCO Kakatiya temple known for floating bricks and intricate dance sculptures.

name: Bhongir Fort
description: Monolithic rock fort near Hyderabad — steep climb and panoramic Deccan views.

name: Udayagiri and Khandagiri Caves
description: Jain rock-cut caves of King Kharavela’s era — Hathigumpha inscription site.

name: Vishwa Shanti Stupa Rajgir
description: Peace pagoda on Ratnagiri hill with ropeway — Buddhist circuit near Nalanda.

name: Vikramshila University Ruins
description: Ruins of an ancient Buddhist university founded by King Dharmapala.

name: Baidyanath Jyotirlinga Deoghar
description: One of the twelve Jyotirlingas — major Shravan pilgrimage centre of eastern India.

name: Triveni Sangam Prayagraj
description: Confluence of Ganga, Yamuna and mythical Saraswati — site of Kumbh Mela.

name: Ram Mandir Ayodhya
description: Grand temple of Lord Rama at Ram Janmabhoomi — major pilgrimage destination of India.

name: Krishna Janmabhoomi Mathura
description: Sacred complex marking the birthplace of Lord Krishna on the Yamuna.

name: Banke Bihari Temple
description: Beloved Krishna temple of Vrindavan — lively lanes of Braj bhakti culture.

name: Kempty Falls
description: Popular cascade near Mussoorie — picnic pools and hill-station day trip.

name: The Ridge Shimla
description: Open mall and colonial heart of Shimla with Christ Church and Himalayan views.

name: Jakhu Temple
description: Hilltop Hanuman temple with giant statue above Shimla — ropeway or steep walk.

name: Tsuglagkhang Complex
description: Dalai Lama’s temple complex in McLeod Ganj — Tibetan Buddhism hub of Dharamshala.

name: Durpin Monastery Kalimpong
description: Hill monastery with 108 prayer wheels and views over Teesta valley.

name: Sajnekhali Watch Tower
description: Main interpretation centre and watchtower for Sundarbans mangrove safari circuit.

name: Belur Math
description: Headquarters of Ramakrishna Mission on the Hooghly — serene architecture blending faiths.

name: Ross Island Andaman
description: Abandoned British administrative island with ruins reclaimed by forest near Port Blair.

name: Baratang Limestone Caves
description: Mangrove boat ride to limestone caves — unique Andaman nature excursion.

name: Agatti Lagoon
description: Turquoise coral lagoon of Lakshadweep — snorkelling gateway island.

name: Bangaram Island
description: Uninhabited coral island near Agatti — quiet lagoon for diving and kayaking.

name: Motí Daman Fort
description: Portuguese seaside fort with churches and ramparts in Moti Daman.

name: Sirpur Heritage Site
description: Ancient Buddhist and Hindu ruins on the Mahanadi — quiet archaeological destination.

name: Lodhi Garden
description: Historic park with Lodhi-era tombs under trees — favourite city walk of Delhi.
